consistency.summary: The data appears internally consistent, mapping standard D&D 5e magic item attributes.
consistency.details: Name ('Luck Blade'), Type ('Weapon (any sword)'), Rarity ('Legendary'), and Attunement requirement match the descriptive text and mechanical effects.

quality.summary: High quality. The description clearly outlines all mechanical benefits.
quality.details: Includes standard bonuses (+1 attack/damage, +1 saving throws), a limited-use ability ('Luck' reroll), and a powerful charge-based ability ('Wish'). The source/rarity notation is slightly redundant but present.

mechanics.summary: Mechanically robust, featuring multiple layered benefits.

details.base_bonus: +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.
details.passive_bonus: +1 bonus to saving throws while on person.

limited_use_ability.name: Luck
limited_use_ability.effect: Reroll one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw (must use second roll).

charge_ability.name: Wish
charge_ability.casting_mechanic: Action to expend 1 charge to cast _wish_ spell.
charge_ability.reset: Property lost if no charges remain; uses are limited to the number of charges remaining.

potential_issues_or_notes: The charge count (1d4 - 1) means the item might start with 0 charges, rendering the _Wish_ property unusable immediately. This is standard for this specific item but worth noting.
The description uses markdown formatting (e.g., `_Wish_`, `[_wish_](#wish)`), which should be stripped or standardized if this data were being ported to a plain text system.
